jokes.text: Well, when I'm nervous... I put a bag over my head and breathe very deeply.
jokes.setup: Ashley offers advice for handling nervousness
jokes.punchline: Her solution is to put a bag over her head
jokes.explanation: Unexpected and absurd advice that fits Ashley's earnest but naive character perfectly

jokes.text: By the time I regain consciousness... I've forgotten everything I was worried about.
jokes.setup: Ashley explains how putting a bag over her head helps
jokes.punchline: She passes out from lack of oxygen
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that takes the absurd premise to its logical conclusion with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: That's how I eat them. [ASHLEY SIGHS]
jokes.setup: Will claims he wasn't biting his nails
jokes.punchline: He's clearly eating his fingernails, Ashley reacts with disgust
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy that works but isn't particularly clever or memorable

jokes.text: So what, you and Lisa have no place to live. And no money in the bank. And a crummy dead-end job. Heh.
jokes.setup: Ashley tries to reassure Will about his marriage worries
jokes.punchline: She lists increasingly dire problems
jokes.explanation: Good escalating structure that builds tension before the reveal

jokes.text: Oh, wait. That would be me.
jokes.setup: Ashley lists problems about Will and Lisa
jokes.punchline: She realizes she's describing her own situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy in the punchline, though the setup is somewhat predictable

jokes.text: You'll always be able to make a living. You do own a squeegee, don't you?
jokes.setup: Ashley continues trying to reassure Will about making a living
jokes.punchline: Suggests he can work as a window washer
jokes.explanation: Decent class humor but fairly predictable and not particularly sharp

jokes.text: What I need is somebody to just walk in and say... You know what? I just made a bundle on the stock market. Ha. Now I just need something to invest it in. CHORUS [SINGING]: Hallelujah, hallelujah
jokes.setup: Will wishes for an investor to appear
jokes.punchline: Gospel chorus appears on cue as if answering his prayer
jokes.explanation: Creative meta-theatrical moment that breaks the fourth wall effectively

jokes.text: Well, first I tell him that I love him. Then I hug him so tight his wallet falls out and I kick it under the couch. Ha.
jokes.setup: Will asks how Hilary gets money from Uncle Phil
jokes.punchline: She describes an elaborate pickpocketing scheme disguised as affection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary character moment - manipulative but delivered with complete innocence

jokes.text: So you hug him just to get money? That is low.
jokes.setup: Will expresses moral outrage at Hilary's scheme
jokes.punchline: Immediately after saying it's low, he does exactly the same thing
jokes.explanation: Good hypocrisy reveal that shows Will's opportunistic nature

jokes.text: PHIL: Well... WILL: Give me a hug, you big bear.
jokes.setup: Will just criticized Hilary for hugging Phil for money
jokes.punchline: Phil enters and Will immediately does the exact same thing
jokes.explanation: Immediate payoff to the setup creates good comedic timing

jokes.text: - Stupid? Ha-ha-ha. PHIL: Bingo.
jokes.setup: Phil searches for a word to describe Will's investment ideas
jokes.punchline: Carlton cheerfully suggests 'stupid' and Phil agrees
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Carlton's earnestness and family dynamics

jokes.text: I don't know. Drugstore was closed that weekend?
jokes.setup: Carlton brags about giving Phil financial advice
jokes.punchline: Will implies Carlton was an accidental conception
jokes.explanation: Clever birth control joke that's sharp without being too crude

jokes.text: Yes. But your father never heard about fried-chicken-flavored aspirin.
jokes.setup: Phil says there's no such thing as a bad idea
jokes.punchline: References Will's absurd previous invention
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd invention name that's memorable and character-specific

jokes.text: And let's not forget about his beepers that cough.
jokes.setup: Phil continues listing Will's bad ideas
jokes.punchline: Another absurd invention concept
jokes.explanation: Good absurd concept but not as strong as the previous example

jokes.text: See, you're sitting in a movie theater, your beeper go off. Instead of hearing beep, beep, beep, beep, you hear: [COUGHING AND SNORTING]
jokes.setup: Will explains the logic of coughing beepers
jokes.punchline: Makes horrible coughing sounds to demonstrate
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that works but the logic doesn't quite hold up

jokes.text: See? - That's a lot less annoying, right?
jokes.setup: Will demonstrates horrible coughing sounds
jokes.punchline: Claims this is less annoying than beeping
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will logic - completely wrong but delivered with total confidence

jokes.text: Mm, mm, mm. Little Lance Ito. Who'd have thought it?
jokes.setup: Phil says to present things professionally
jokes.punchline: Will compares Phil to the O.J. Simpson trial judge
jokes.explanation: Dated pop culture reference that feels lazy and doesn't add much to the character

jokes.text: Get off me. I'm only gonna say this twice.
jokes.setup: Jazz said he'd only say 'get off me' once
jokes.punchline: Immediately says it again and updates his count
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Jazz's stubborn persistence with nice escalation

jokes.text: Uh, go with the original. Extra crispy is overrated.
jokes.setup: Phil says he's making a crucial decision
jokes.punchline: Will assumes it's about fried chicken
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays on stereotypes but stays character-appropriate

jokes.text: [IN JAMAICAN ACCENT] Uncle Phil, mon... how would you like to live in an island paradise?
jokes.setup: Jazz and Will plan to pitch an investment
jokes.punchline: Jazz does an over-the-top Jamaican accent
jokes.explanation: Stereotypical accent work that feels dated and not particularly clever

jokes.text: The sun from your melon be blinding the little children.
jokes.setup: Jazz continues his Jamaican pitch
jokes.punchline: Insults Phil's bald head in fake Jamaican
jokes.explanation: Creative insult about Phil's baldness but the accent gimmick weakens it

jokes.text: Well... Jazz lives here, Uncle Phil. Oh, that's very different. I know I'm not interested.
jokes.setup: Will tries to sell Phil on the building
jokes.punchline: Phil immediately loses interest when he hears Jazz lives there
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Phil's relationship with Jazz, good timing

jokes.text: I lived in a strip mall, Mr. Banks. And I don't need to tell you how hard it is to take a bath in the men's room sink.
jokes.setup: Jazz mentions living in a strip mall
jokes.punchline: Reveals the uncomfortable detail about bathing in public restrooms
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that reveals Jazz's dire living conditions with specificity

jokes.text: Oh, well, I'm sorry, Whoopi.
jokes.setup: Will complains Phil isn't taking him seriously
jokes.punchline: Phil compares Will to Whoopi Goldberg
jokes.explanation: Lazy pop culture reference that doesn't add much comedic value

jokes.text: But I can assure you that Donald Trump does not negotiate business deals... playing a ukulele and wearing a Bullwinkle hat.
jokes.setup: Phil explains proper business presentation
jokes.punchline: Points out Will's ridiculous appearance during the pitch
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy reference that makes the absurdity of their presentation clear

jokes.text: - Danish? - Only on my mother's side.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey offers Will a Danish pastry
jokes.punchline: Will thinks he's asking about his heritage
jokes.explanation: Perfect wordplay misdirection with excellent economy and delivery

jokes.text: You know, it's amazing because just last night... you were saying how stupid this was. It must have morphed.
jokes.setup: Phil praised the same presentation he called stupid
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically suggests it 'morphed'
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response that highlights Phil's hypocrisy

jokes.text: You backpedal any faster, Uncle Phil... you're gonna moonwalk right through that wall.
jokes.setup: Phil is backtracking on his criticism
jokes.punchline: Compares it to Michael Jackson's moonwalk
jokes.explanation: Clever visual metaphor combining backpedaling with moonwalking, very quotable

jokes.text: I mean, you spend more than that on Milk Duds. You know, in the course of a year. You know.
jokes.setup: Will asks for a small down payment
jokes.punchline: Compares it to Phil's candy budget, then weakly qualifies it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's desperation and weak sales technique

jokes.text: You know, Will, you backpedal pretty well yourself.
jokes.setup: Will just accused Phil of backpedaling
jokes.punchline: Phil turns it back on Will who's now backpedaling himself
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Phil can give as good as he gets

jokes.text: I told you to get a big-screen TV.
jokes.setup: Phil complains about substandard living conditions
jokes.punchline: Jazz thinks he's complaining about not having a big TV
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jazz's priorities and misunderstanding

jokes.text: Excuse me, my beeper. [PHIL COUGHS]
jokes.setup: Phil's beeper goes off
jokes.punchline: It coughs instead of beeping, as Will predicted
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff to Will's earlier invention, great timing and execution

jokes.text: Find something nice to say about that. I think I killed a rat.
jokes.setup: Will challenges Carlton to find something positive
jokes.punchline: Carlton's positive is that he killed a pest
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor that shows how bad the building is through Carlton's perspective

jokes.text: [MUFFLED] Could someone grab the cappuccino maker?
jokes.setup: Phil claims he knows how to rough it
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks for his cappuccino maker while his voice is muffled
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Phil's inability to actually rough it

jokes.text: Well, Uncle Phil, the only way it could get rough now... is if Geoffrey forgot your croissants.
jokes.setup: Phil is trying to rough it with a cappuccino maker
jokes.punchline: Will suggests croissants would make it truly rough
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the luxury vs. roughing it theme

jokes.text: Now our little penthouse got central air. Whoosh.
jokes.setup: Will tries to find a bright side
jokes.punchline: Calls the hole in the ceiling 'central air'
jokes.explanation: Good positive spin on a terrible situation, characteristic Will optimism

jokes.text: And so will this. [CRUNCHING] You allow pets.
jokes.setup: Will continues finding positives
jokes.punchline: Points to vermin as 'pets' while eating something crunchy
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor that makes the gross situation funny

jokes.text: - Knock, knock. - Who's there? The head of the tenants association. That's me.
jokes.setup: Jazz starts a knock-knock joke
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's the person Phil needs to meet with
jokes.explanation: Clever use of knock-knock format to reveal plot information

jokes.text: After you put a couple of million dollars in this place, it'll be worth thousands.
jokes.setup: Jazz tries to be encouraging about the investment
jokes.punchline: Suggests spending millions to make it worth thousands
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd math that perfectly captures the terrible investment with great economy

jokes.text: Somebody keep making them damn Chevy Chase movies.
jokes.setup: Jazz reads complaints list
jokes.punchline: One complaint is about Chevy Chase movies
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about bad movies that's moderately funny but feels disconnected

jokes.text: Sticky stuff on the stairs tastes bitter.
jokes.setup: Jazz reads building complaints
jokes.punchline: Someone is tasting mysterious sticky substances
jokes.explanation: Disturbing and funny complaint that reveals how bad the building is

jokes.text: No. He wants you to call the idiot... who keeps greenlighting all those Chevy Chase movies.
jokes.setup: Will asks if Carlton wants him to call repairmen
jokes.punchline: Carlton sarcastically refers back to the Chevy Chase complaint
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Carlton can be sarcastic when frustrated

jokes.text: Oh, I bet you are, Carlton. Because you can't kiss his butt from here.
jokes.setup: Carlton says he'll follow Phil closely
jokes.punchline: Will suggests it's because Carlton can't kiss up from a distance
jokes.explanation: Standard Will-Carlton dynamic but feels a bit forced here

jokes.text: What kind of idiot would patch the roof with newspaper? [PHIL SCREAMING]
jokes.setup: Carlton questions who would patch a roof with newspaper
jokes.punchline: Phil immediately falls through said newspaper patch
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and structure - question immediately answered by physical comedy

jokes.text: Oh. Didn't you hear? Their new hits collection comes out next week.
jokes.setup: Will asks about the carpenters
jokes.punchline: Carlton thinks he means The Carpenters music group
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - earnest misunderstanding that fits his character completely

jokes.text: [IMITATING CARLTON] Oh. Well, jeez Louise... we better rush down and wait in line right now.
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions The Carpenters' hits collection
jokes.punchline: Will mocks Carlton's enthusiasm with an impression
jokes.explanation: Good Will impression of Carlton that captures his earnestness

jokes.text: Bunny rabbit. Ha, ha. Look. An American eagle with one wing.
jokes.setup: Carlton is supposed to be helping with electrical work
jokes.punchline: He's making shadow puppets instead
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton getting distracted like a child

jokes.text: [SCREAMING]
jokes.setup: Carlton touches the electrical box despite warnings
jokes.punchline: Both get electrocuted
jokes.explanation: Predictable but effective physical comedy payoff to the setup

jokes.text: Oh, at own risk. Sorry.
jokes.setup: Carlton just electrocuted himself and Will
jokes.punchline: Casually corrects his misreading after the damage is done
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - completely missing the gravity of the situation with polite understatement

jokes.text: In fact, this week was kind of invigorating. Which reminds me, I think I'm gonna go freshen up. I think I saw a hydrant on the corner and now I know how to use a wrench.
jokes.setup: Phil claims the week was invigorating
jokes.punchline: Plans to wash at a fire hydrant
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Phil adapting to rough conditions

jokes.text: Well, you got me there.
jokes.setup: Will asks why Lisa wants to marry him
jokes.punchline: Phil admits he can't think of a reason
jokes.explanation: Good honest moment that gets a laugh while advancing character development

jokes.text: You used to be a lot better at these little talks, Uncle Phil.
jokes.setup: Phil gives unhelpful advice
jokes.punchline: Will comments on Phil's declining pep talk skills
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary on the show's own convention of heartfelt talks

jokes.text: Hey, man, don't you think I'm a little old for a spanking?
jokes.setup: Phil threatens to 'go there' with Will
jokes.punchline: Will thinks he means physical punishment
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that fits the family dynamic

jokes.text: And they say guys can't express their true feelings. You know what I'm saying? Hah, hah. That's ridiculous, isn't it? Ha-ha-ha.
jokes.setup: Will and Phil have an awkward emotional moment
jokes.punchline: They joke about men not expressing feelings while doing exactly that
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment about masculine emotional expression that feels authentic

jokes.text: Oh, 'let's jet.' That was hip. Did you hurt yourself?
jokes.setup: Will says 'let's jet'
jokes.punchline: Phil mocks Will's attempt at being cool
jokes.explanation: Good generational comedy with Phil getting the last word

jokes.text: Damn, a coat of paint in a roach motel and you're all over yourself.
jokes.setup: Phil expects thanks from Jazz
jokes.punchline: Jazz sarcastically describes the minimal improvements
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic description that captures the building's condition

jokes.text: Yeah, an offer to withhold my rent until I can come up with it.
jokes.setup: Jazz offers to withhold rent
jokes.punchline: Reveals he means he can't pay it anyway
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that reveals Jazz's financial situation while sounding official

jokes.text: [YELLS] [JAZZ THUDDING] Ooh. My God. Uh, that's okay, Uncle Phil. His rent check would have bounced too.
jokes.setup: Jazz walks down the hazardous stairs
jokes.punchline: Falls through, Will makes joke about his bad check
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy ending with callback to Jazz's financial problems
